title: mdtoc slugger tournament: candidate-agnostic golden vectors
description: Owns projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py. Define SLUG_VECTORS and DEDUP_SEQUENCES of known GitHub anchor behavior plus run_against(slugify) that threads a shared seen dict. Candidate-agnostic: imports NO candidate. Created FIRST so the three slugger candidates can conform to one interface. Stdlib only.
acceptance_criteria: Owns EXACTLY: projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py. Touch no other file. This is the CANDIDATE-AGNOSTIC task and MUST be done before any slugger candidate starts (it defines the shared interface + golden data).

Defines the shared interface in the module docstring: slugify(text: str, seen: dict) -> str returns a GitHub-style anchor; seen maps base-slug -> count of prior uses and is MUTATED to dedupe (first occurrence 'foo', then 'foo-1', 'foo-2', ...).

SLUG_VECTORS: list of (input_text, expected_slug) single-shot cases (each evaluated with a fresh empty seen) covering: lowercasing; spaces->hyphens; punctuation stripped; unicode word chars RETAINED (an accented example e.g. Café and a CJK example e.g. 日本語); emoji REMOVED; underscores RETAINED (snake_case).

DEDUP_SEQUENCES: list of {"inputs": [...], "expected": [...]} covering duplicate dedup threaded through one shared seen dict, e.g. ['Setup','Setup','Setup'] -> ['setup','setup-1','setup-2'] and ['Foo','Bar','Foo'] -> ['foo','bar','foo-1'].

run_against(slugify_callable) -> (passed:int, total:int, failures:list): evaluates every SLUG_VECTOR with a fresh seen and every DEDUP_SEQUENCE with one shared seen; returns counts + a list of (case, expected, got) failures. Imports NO candidate module.
Each expected value is annotated with a comment citing the GitHub / github-slugger rule it reflects (so candidates and the verdict can trust the ground truth).
stdlib only; `python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'projects/mdtoc/candidates'); import vectors"` imports cleanly.

SHARED CONVENTIONS (all mdtoc tasks): Python 3.9+ stdlib ONLY, no pip deps. Package dir projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/ (namespace package; __init__.py is created only by T-028, so test files must add projects/mdtoc/ to sys.path and import as `from mdtoc.parser import ...`). Tests use unittest and must pass via `python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc`. No file listed here may be created/edited by any other task (disjoint ownership).

notes.note: plan: write candidate-agnostic projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py with slugify(text,seen) interface docstring, SLUG_VECTORS, DEDUP_SEQUENCES, run_against(slugify_callable)

expected: candidate-agnostic golden vectors module importing no candidate, with slugify(text,seen)->str interface documented, SLUG_VECTORS (11) covering lowercasing/spaces->hyphens/punctuation-strip/double-hyphen-via-stripped-symbol/unicode-Latin-retained/unicode-CJK-retained/emoji-removed/underscore-retained/existing-hyphens/digits/parens/mixed-case, DEDUP_SEQUENCES (4, 13 cases) covering same-slug repeats, unrelated-slug non-interference, case-insensitive collision, and long repeat chains, plus run_against(slugify_callable)->(passed,total,failures). ACTUAL: python3 projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py -> self-check OK, 11 SLUG_VECTORS + 4 DEDUP_SEQUENCES (13 cases) = 24 total golden cases, exit 0. python3 -c import check -> 'import OK'. goal_mode.py run --cmd 'python3 projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py' -> GOAL REACHED iteration 1/3. Extra internal validation (scratchpad only, not committed): wrote a throwaway reference slugify implementing the cited html-pipeline algorithm (lower + strip non [\w\- ] via re.UNICODE + space->hyphen + seen-count dedup) and ran vectors.run_against(ref_slugify) -> passed=24 total=24, 0 failures, confirming internal self-consistency of the golden data before handoff.

HANDOFF -> verifier: REPLAY: (1) python3 projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py -> exits 0, prints 'self-check OK', 11 SLUG_VECTORS + 4 DEDUP_SEQUENCES (13 cases) = 24 total. (2) python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'projects/mdtoc/candidates'); import vectors" -> imports cleanly (no side effects at import time; self-check only runs under __main__ guard). (3) python3 .harness/bin/goal_mode.py run --cmd "python3 projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py" -> GOAL REACHED iteration 1/3. (4) Interface check: vectors.run_against is defined as run_against(slugify_callable) -> (passed:int, total:int, failures:list[(case,expected,got)]) matching the module docstring's slugify(text:str, seen:dict)->str contract; SLUG_VECTORS entries are evaluated with a fresh seen={} each, DEDUP_SEQUENCES entries are evaluated with one shared seen dict threaded across that sequence's inputs. (5) Ground-truth sourcing: every SLUG_VECTORS/DEDUP_SEQUENCES entry has an inline comment citing the GitHub html-pipeline TocFilter#generate_id algorithm (downcase -> strip non [word|hyphen|space] chars -> space->hyphen) as the rule it encodes, including the well-known 'Foo & Bar' -> 'foo--bar' double-hyphen quirk (punctuation removal does not collapse surrounding whitespace) and case-insensitive dedup collision ('Config'/'CONFIG'/'config' all -> base 'config'). (6) Self-verified before handoff (not part of the shipped file): wrote a throwaway reference slugify in the scratchpad implementing exactly the cited algorithm and ran vectors.run_against(ref_slugify) -> passed=24 total=24, zero failures -- confirms the golden data is internally consistent with its own cited ground-truth rule, independent of any of the three future candidates. File touches EXACTLY projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py, imports no candidate module, stdlib only (re/typing not even needed at runtime beyond typing hints from __future__/typing). One scope note for the verifier: the delegating prompt additionally suggested leading/trailing-space handling, punctuation-only headings, and code-span-stripping vectors; I deliberately omitted those because they are NOT in T-023.json's acceptance_criteria and their correct ground truth is ambiguous outside a parser context (e.g. whether slugify() or parser.py is responsible for trimming/code-fence handling) -- see my final report for the full rationale.

VERIFIED: replayed python3 projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py -> self-check OK, 11 SLUG_VECTORS + 4 DEDUP_SEQUENCES (13 cases) = 24 total, exit 0; clean import OK. DATA AUDIT: wrote an INDEPENDENT html-pipeline reference (re '[^\\w\\- ]' + lower + space->hyphen + naive base->count dedup); all 24 vectors pass. Unicode categories hand-verified: e-acute(Ll)/CJK+kana(Lo)/underscore(Pc) match \\w -> retained; emoji U+2728(So) does NOT match \\w -> stripped ('Great(emoji)News Update'->'greatnews-update' correct); & . ( ) ! , stripped. 'Foo & Bar'->'foo--bar' CORRECT (space-&-space, only & removed, no whitespace collapse -> double hyphen). 'Section 2.1 Overview'->'section-21-overview' and 'Foo (Bar) Baz'->'foo-bar-baz' correct (no residue hyphens). Cafe vector uses PRECOMPOSED e-acute (U+00E9) consistently in input+expected (both NFC) -> no normalization trap. Case-insensitive dedup 'Config'/'CONFIG'/'config'->config/config-1/config-2 correct (dedup keys off the lowercased slug). Candidate-agnostic (imports only __future__/typing, no candidate/mdtoc), stdlib only. Discipline: worker-3 locked+released ONLY vectors.py, went in_progress, handed off to verifier (NOT self-done). COVERAGE NOTE FOR T-027: docstring pins a NAIVE base->count dedup; this DIVERGES from real github-slugger on the UNTESTED 'produced-slug collides with a later base' case, e.g. ['Foo','Foo','Foo-1'] -> naive ['foo','foo-1','foo-1'] (COLLISION) vs GitHub ['foo','foo-1','foo-1-1']. All 3 candidates code to the naive docstring so they converge on tested inputs, but T-027 should add a collision-edge vector to prevent a false 24/24 tie masking real-input disagreement (and note a faithful github-slugger port SHOULD fail the emoji vector). Minor: docstring prose overstates that github-slugger 'reproduces' html-pipeline exactly (it differs on emoji + dedup collisions) - prose only, no data error.
